metadata.title: FedCloud Formal Verification Gateway — System Security Plan

system-characteristics.description: An automated compliance verification system that uses the Lean 4 interactive theorem prover to mathematically prove that cloud infrastructure configurations satisfy federal security requirements. Replaces narrative compliance documentation with deterministic formal proofs.

information-types.title: Infrastructure Configuration Data
information-types.description: Cloud resource configurations, IAM policies, encryption settings, and network topology metadata.

authorization-boundary.description: The authorization boundary encompasses the formal verification gateway engine, the state ingestion pipeline, the JSON-to-Lean translator, the OSCAL synthesis service, and all supporting infrastructure within the security operations VPC.

components.title: Formal Verification Gateway Engine
components.description: Lean 4 theorem prover kernel that evaluates security invariants against infrastructure state. Pending initial verification run.

components.title: State Ingestion Pipeline
components.description: Event-driven service that collects infrastructure state from AWS Config, CloudTrail, EventBridge, and application APIs.

components.title: OSCAL Synthesis Service
components.description: Converts verification receipts into machine-readable OSCAL SSP updates and Assessment Results.

control-implementation.description: Controls are implemented through formal mathematical verification. Each control family maps to an invariant theorem that is continuously evaluated by the Lean 4 proof engine.
